_comment: Example Codex CLI hooks configuration for security enforcement. Deploy alongside your .codex/config.toml.

SessionStart.name: validate-environment
SessionStart.description: Verify security prerequisites before the session begins
args: set -euo pipefail; ERRORS=0; if [ "${CODEX_SANDBOX_MODE:-}" = 'full-auto' ]; then echo 'ERROR: full-auto sandbox mode is not permitted' >&2; ERRORS=$((ERRORS+1)); fi; if ! command -v semgrep >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'WARN: semgrep not installed — pre-commit security scanning unavailable' >&2; fi; if ! command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'WARN: gitleaks not installed — secret scanning unavailable' >&2; fi; if ! git config user.email >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'ERROR: git user.email not configured — commits will not be attributed' >&2; ERRORS=$((ERRORS+1)); fi; if [ "$ERRORS" -gt 0 ]; then exit 1; fi; echo 'Environment validation passed'

SessionStart.name: log-session-start
SessionStart.description: Record session start for audit trail

PreToolUse.name: block-destructive-commands
PreToolUse.description: Prevent dangerous shell commands from executing
args: CMD="$CODEX_TOOL_ARGS"; BLOCKED_PATTERNS=('rm -rf /' 'rm -rf ~' 'rm -rf $HOME' 'mkfs\.' 'dd if=' ':(){' 'chmod 777' 'chmod -R 777' 'sudo ' 'su -' 'su root'); for pattern in "${BLOCKED_PATTERNS[@]}"; do if echo "$CMD" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then echo "BLOCKED: Command matches destructive pattern: $pattern" >&2; exit 1; fi; done; echo 'Command approved'

PreToolUse.name: block-network-tools
PreToolUse.description: Block network access commands as defense-in-depth (supplements sandbox)
args: CMD="$CODEX_TOOL_ARGS"; NETWORK_CMDS=('curl ' 'wget ' 'nc ' 'ncat ' 'netcat ' 'ssh ' 'scp ' 'rsync ' 'ftp ' 'telnet ' 'nmap '); for ncmd in "${NETWORK_CMDS[@]}"; do if echo "$CMD" | grep -qiE "(^|[;&|])\s*$ncmd"; then echo "BLOCKED: Network command not permitted: $ncmd" >&2; exit 1; fi; done; echo 'No network commands detected'

PreToolUse.name: block-sensitive-path-access
PreToolUse.description: Prevent read/write access to credential and config directories
args: CMD="$CODEX_TOOL_ARGS"; SENSITIVE_PATHS=('.ssh/' '.aws/' '.gnupg/' '.config/gcloud' '.kube/' '.env' 'id_rsa' 'id_ed25519' 'credentials' '.netrc'); for spath in "${SENSITIVE_PATHS[@]}"; do if echo "$CMD" | grep -qi "$spath"; then echo "BLOCKED: Access to sensitive path not permitted: $spath" >&2; exit 1; fi; done; echo 'No sensitive path access detected'

PreToolUse.name: block-install-commands
PreToolUse.description: Block package installation to prevent supply chain attacks
args: CMD="$CODEX_TOOL_ARGS"; INSTALL_PATTERNS=('pip install' 'npm install' 'yarn add' 'gem install' 'cargo install' 'go install' 'apt install' 'apt-get install' 'brew install' 'curl.*|.*sh' 'curl.*|.*bash'); for pattern in "${INSTALL_PATTERNS[@]}"; do if echo "$CMD" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then echo "BLOCKED: Package installation not permitted without approval: $pattern" >&2; exit 1; fi; done; echo 'No install commands detected'

PostToolUse.name: audit-log-tool-use
PostToolUse.description: Log every tool execution for audit trail

PostToolUse.name: scan-output-for-secrets
PostToolUse.description: Check tool output for accidentally exposed secrets
args: OUTPUT="${CODEX_TOOL_OUTPUT:-}"; SECRET_PATTERNS=('sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}' 'AKIA[A-Z0-9]{16}' 'ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}' 'glpat-[a-zA-Z0-9\-]{20}' '-----BEGIN.*PRIVATE KEY-----' 'xox[bpras]-[a-zA-Z0-9\-]+'); for pattern in "${SECRET_PATTERNS[@]}"; do if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then echo "WARNING: Possible secret detected in tool output (pattern: $pattern)" >&2; echo "{\"event\": \"secret_detected\", \"timestamp\": \"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\", \"pattern\": \"$pattern\", \"tool\": \"${CODEX_TOOL_NAME:-unknown}\"}" >> "${CODEX_AUDIT_LOG:-/tmp/codex-audit.jsonl}"; fi; done

PostToolUse.name: check-file-permissions
PostToolUse.description: Verify that written files do not have overly permissive permissions
args: if [ "${CODEX_TOOL_NAME:-}" = 'file_write' ] && [ -n "${CODEX_TOOL_TARGET:-}" ]; then PERMS=$(stat -c '%a' "$CODEX_TOOL_TARGET" 2>/dev/null || stat -f '%Lp' "$CODEX_TOOL_TARGET" 2>/dev/null); if [ "$PERMS" = '777' ] || [ "$PERMS" = '776' ] || [ "$PERMS" = '766' ]; then echo "WARNING: File $CODEX_TOOL_TARGET has overly permissive permissions: $PERMS" >&2; fi; fi

SessionEnd.name: generate-session-summary
SessionEnd.description: Create an audit summary of the session
args: AUDIT_LOG="${CODEX_AUDIT_LOG:-/tmp/codex-audit.jsonl}"; if [ -f "$AUDIT_LOG" ]; then TOOL_COUNT=$(grep -c '"event": "tool_use"' "$AUDIT_LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); SECRET_COUNT=$(grep -c '"event": "secret_detected"' "$AUDIT_LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); echo "{\"event\": \"session_end\", \"timestamp\": \"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\", \"session_id\": \"${CODEX_SESSION_ID:-unknown}\", \"total_tool_uses\": $TOOL_COUNT, \"secrets_detected\": $SECRET_COUNT}" >> "$AUDIT_LOG"; echo "Session complete: $TOOL_COUNT tool uses, $SECRET_COUNT secret warnings"; fi

environment_variables.CODEX_AUDIT_LOG: Path to the JSONL audit log file (default: /tmp/codex-audit.jsonl)
environment_variables.CODEX_SESSION_ID: Unique session identifier (set by Codex CLI)
environment_variables.CODEX_SANDBOX_MODE: Current sandbox mode (set by Codex CLI)
environment_variables.CODEX_APPROVAL_POLICY: Current approval policy (set by Codex CLI)
environment_variables.CODEX_TOOL_NAME: Name of the tool being executed (set by Codex CLI)
environment_variables.CODEX_TOOL_ARGS: Arguments passed to the tool (set by Codex CLI)
environment_variables.CODEX_TOOL_OUTPUT: Output from the tool execution (PostToolUse only)
environment_variables.CODEX_TOOL_EXIT_CODE: Exit code from the tool execution (PostToolUse only)
environment_variables.CODEX_TOOL_TARGET: Target file path for file operations (set by Codex CLI)

customization: Adjust BLOCKED_PATTERNS in block-destructive-commands to match your organization's policy
Add organization-specific sensitive paths to block-sensitive-path-access
Configure CODEX_AUDIT_LOG to point to a centralized log location or SIEM forwarder
Adjust timeout_ms values based on your environment's performance characteristics
Set on_failure to 'abort' for critical security hooks, 'warn' for advisory hooks

on_failure_options.abort: Stop the operation — use for security-critical hooks
on_failure_options.warn: Log a warning and continue — use for audit and advisory hooks
on_failure_options.ignore: Silently continue — NOT recommended for security hooks
